reasoning:
The benchmark value for comparison is the most recent known value as of the forecast date (2026-02-15). According to the FRED WEI series up to 2026-02-15, the latest observable value is 2.90 (for the week ending 2026-02-14). Historically, the WEI over the previous year has ranged mostly between 1.88 and 2.61, with a mean near 2.30. The value of 2.90 represents a massive spike and is the highest value in the past 12 months.

The WEI tracks highly volatile high-frequency indicators (like weekly UI claims, electricity load, retail sales), which are prone to significant mean-reversion following temporary shocks (such as a severe weather event increasing energy load or shifting holiday sales). For example, past spikes above 2.50 (e.g., 2.61 in Mar and Aug 2025, or 2.58 in Apr 2025) were all followed by rapid declines within 1 to 2 weeks (dropping to levels like 2.09, 1.93, 2.32, etc.).

Given this strong pattern of mean-reversion, the probability of the index exceeding the high-water mark of 2.90 by the first resolution date (2026-02-22) is very low. For the subsequent date (2026-03-17), the probability remains low as the series will likely return to its baseline (around 2.30), but uncertainty increases with the length of the forecast horizon, pulling the probability slightly closer to 50%.
